_comment: SpeciGo LIS Integration Engine - Analyser Instance Config (Mindray BS-230 over TCP/IP)
_confirmed_from: BS-230 Operator's Manual V8.0 (2022-12), section 8.6 LIS setup

connection._comment: BS-230 in TCP/IP mode dials OUT to the LIS host on the configured port. The engine binds a TCP listener and accepts the analyser as the connecting peer. On the analyser side (Utility > System Setup > Host F5), set Transport=Network/TCP, Host IP = this workstation's LAN IP, and Host Port = the value below. 'host' here is the listen interface; use '0.0.0.0' to accept connections on all NICs, or pin to a specific IP for stricter binding.

protocol._comment: BS-230 supports both ASTM E1394 and HL7 per manual section 1.4.8/1.4.9. v1 of this engine implements ASTM E1394 (LIS2-A2) only — same wire format as the serial variant. Set Protocol=ASTM 1394, Mode=Bidirectional or Unidirectional in the analyser's Host Communication Parameters window.
protocol._maxFrameBytes_comment: ASTM E1394 spec is 240, but real Mindray BS-series firmware emits H records of 241+ bytes and occasionally packs multiple records into a single STX..ETX frame (no ETB segmentation). Strict 240-byte enforcement causes spurious NAKs against conformant devices, so we use a generous soft cap that still protects against runaway peers.
protocol._unidirectional_comment: Per BS-230 manual §8.6.2, 'Unidirectional' on the analyser only disables sample-program downloads from LIS — the ASTM E1394 transport-layer ACK/NAK handshake is unchanged in both modes and the analyser ENQ has a ~3s ACK timeout. This flag therefore ONLY relaxes the ENQ-precedes-STX rule (some firmware sends STX directly in true unidirectional mode); ACK/NAK is always sent. Safe to leave true even when the analyser is set to Bidirectional. Also auto-enabled if lis_settings.mode == 'Unidirectional'.

lis_settings._comment: Mirror the values configured in the BS-230 Host Communication Parameters window (Utility > System Setup > Host F5). See manual section 8.6.2 Table 8.2.

message_filters._comment: Process patient results only for Phase 1. QC/calibration enabled in future via this flag.

parameter_map._comment: Mindray BS-230 identifies each chemistry in the R record's Universal Test ID component[1] (real firmware) or component[0] (simulator-style). Keys are matched case-insensitively (ParameterMapper uppercases). The first block uses the short Channel No. codes shown on the analyser's Chem screen (docs/research/testlist/, 2026-05-08); the second block uses the FULL English names that the on-site analyser firmware actually transmits in the LIS message (observed 2026-05-08 — e.g. 'Aspartate Aminotransferase' instead of 'AST'). Both must be present because the simulator emits the short form and the real device emits the long form.
parameter_map._long_names_comment: Full English names actually emitted by the on-site BS-230 firmware (component[1] of the R-record Universal Test ID). The 'IBIL-D ?' / 'IBIL-D II' variants both map because the firmware sometimes mangles the roman-numeral II into '?' depending on the locale codepage.
